advantages: Distance-based models (k-NN, k-means, clustering) treat features fairly instead of letting the largest numeric range dominate similarity.
Gradient-based and regularized models converge faster and apply L1/L2 penalties on coefficients that reflect predictive value rather than units.
StandardScaler is simple, interpretable as z-scores, and the default choice for PCA, SVM, and many neural pipelines.
MinMaxScaler produces bounded inputs useful for activations or visualization on a fixed [0, 1] canvas.
disadvantages: Fitting scalers on the full dataset before splitting leaks holdout statistics and inflates offline metrics.
MinMaxScaler is highly sensitive to train-set outliers that set min or max and squash typical values.
StandardScaler still uses mean and std, which outliers can inflate—heavy tails may need robust scalers or transforms first.
Unnecessary scaling on tree-only pipelines adds complexity and another serialized artifact without accuracy benefit.
